characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The leader of Gobotron's military, the Guardians. Note that he'snotpart of the civilian government, which Gobotron does have — that would be Zeemon. Barrier Warrior: Can project a protective force field around himself and his allies, but it takes a lot of energy and he can only maintain it for a short time. Cool Plane: He turns into an F-15 jet. Informed Attribute: Leader-1 is usually treated as a massive threat by the Renegades, to the point of being a borderline priority target for his supposed combat prowess and leadership skills. Unfortunately, he never really shows much of the former, usually getting defeated or captured whenever placed in a combat scenario.However,Renegade Rhetoricactually does show off his combat abilities more often, such as in The Stolen Moon where he defeats 6 Renegades in quick succession entirely on his own However,Renegade Rhetoricactually does show off his combat abilities more often, such as in The Stolen Moon where he defeats 6 Renegades in quick succession entirely on his own

characters.name: Leader- 1
characters.description: The leader of Gobotron's military, the Guardians. Note that he'snotpart of the civilian government, which Gobotron does have — that would be Zeemon. Barrier Warrior: Can project a protective force field around himself and his allies, but it takes a lot of energy and he can only maintain it for a short time. Cool Plane: He turns into an F-15 jet. Informed Attribute: Leader-1 is usually treated as a massive threat by the Renegades, to the point of being a borderline priority target for his supposed combat prowess and leadership skills. Unfortunately, he never really shows much of the former, usually getting defeated or captured whenever placed in a combat scenario.However,Renegade Rhetoricactually does show off his combat abilities more often, such as in The Stolen Moon where he defeats 6 Renegades in quick succession entirely on his own However,Renegade Rhetoricactually does show off his combat abilities more often, such as in The Stolen Moon where he defeats 6 Renegades in quick succession entirely on his own

characters.name: Turbo
characters.description: Boisterous Bruiser: He's the muscle of the Guardians and very jovial. Catchphrase: "It's time togo!" Interspecies Friendship: Forms a friendship with AJ Foster. Red Is Heroic: He is colored red and is one of the good guys.

characters.name: Scooter
characters.description: Character Development: He starts out as a constantly kvetching coward, but he gradually grows out of it as the series goes on. Cowardly Lion: He is completely terrified of fighting the Renegades, but usually pulls through. Heterosexual Life-Partners: He forms a close friendship with the Guardians' human ally Nick Burns. Holographic Disguise: He has a hologram projector, which he can use to disguise himself as a Renegade. Red Is Heroic: He is another Guardian colored red.

characters.name: Zeemon
characters.description: One of the elected civilian leaders of Gobotron, explicitly called a politician. Aliens Never Invented Democracy: Averted. Gobotron has a democracy. The Faceless: His robot form has no proper face, just a windshield. Fictional Disability: He lost the ability to convert (transform) after a battle he was involved in long ago. He could have it repaired, but he chose to leave it disabled, as a reminder of the circumstances of the fight. Reasonable Authority Figure: Can be a little pompous sometimes, but is generally good at his job and respected for it.

characters.name: Cy-Kill
characters.description: Benevolent Boss: He shows more loyalty to his troops than one might expect from an 80's cartoon villain. Big Bad: As leader of the Renegades, he's responsible for just about every conflict the Guardians have to resolve. Big Bad Duumvirate: He teams up with Magmar in theBattle of the Rock Lordsmovie, but ends up betraying him to take the power scepter for himself. TheRenegade RhetoricFacebook posts (which were essentially a quasi-official second season to the cartoon) would later have him reluctantly form another alliance with Magmar in the "Combiner Wars" double-post that served as the series' spiritualGrand Finale. Character Blog: He has a Fun Publications-sanctioned Facebook page calledRenegade Rhetoric(initially a temporary edition ofAsk Vector Primebefore continuing as its own separate Facebook page), which consists mainly of creating posts where he describes the events of episodes from a fictional second season ofChallenge of theGoBots. Evil Laugh: Not manic like Crasher's, but his deep voice still makes it memorable.

characters.name: Cop-Tur
characters.description: Catchphrase: "Sorr-ree," whenever Cy-Kill calls him out on having done something dumb. Which is frequent. Co-Dragons: He and Crasher are the two main minions of Cy-Kill. Dumb Muscle: He's a very dim brute. Early-Installment Weirdness: Cop-Tur's toy bio indicates that he is a cleaver and conniving genius who would beThe Starscreamto Cy-Kill's Megatron, but in the cartoon he is loyalDumb Muscle. Evil Sounds Deep: Has a very low, grumbly voice, sounding like a frog who ate nothing but coffee grounds.

characters.name: Crasher
characters.description: Ax-Crazy: She is the most violent of the Renegades. Breakout Character: These days, she's usually considered the most popular character from the series. TheTransformersfranchise eventually introduced a Decepticon named Fracturebased on her.Though that characterwasintended to actually be a new incarnation of Crasher, but trademark issues left her original name unavailable at the time. By the time she recieved a new toy in theLegacytoyline, Crasher regained her original name. Though that characterwasintended to actually be a new incarnation of Crasher, but trademark issues left her original name unavailable at the time. By the time she recieved a new toy in theLegacytoyline, Crasher regained her original name. Card-Carrying Villain: Doesn't even try to hide how much she enjoys mindless destruction. Co-Dragons: Shares the position of main lackey to Cy-Kill with Cop-Tur.

characters.name: Fi-Tor
characters.description: The Dragon: He is Cy-Kill's official second-in-command, even though he doesn't appear as often as Crasher and Cop-Tur do. Face–Heel Turn: Like Cy-Kill, he used to be a Guardian. Space Fighter: His alt-mode. Token Competent Minion: While Crasher and Cop-Tur are tough opponents and usually serve as Cy-Kill's primary minions, both tend to suffer severe drawbacks. Crasher is too violent and can easily get distracted in her efforts to cause mayhem and her destructive stomp can't really be controlled beyond giving it a general direction. Her eagerness to use said power has been used to bait her into destroying technology that was necessary to Cy-Kill's plans. For his part, Cop-Tur is almost the definition ofDumb Muscleand can quickly find himself out of his depth when "hitting it" no longer works. In contrast, while Fi-Tor ultimately did fail to keep the Guardians from capturing the Renegade stronghold, he'd been placed in charge to keep the Guardians occupied while Cy-Kill assembled his Zod Armada, and after the pilot movie was usually the Renegade that could be counted on to adapt plans and mission parameters as the situation changed. Basically while the lion's share of the Renegades (including their former leader) were cannon fodder, Fi-Tor could be a strategist. Villainous Friendship: With Cy-Kill.

characters.name: Dr. Go
characters.description: Adaptational Name Change: His toy was named "Herr Fiend." Presumably this is why he was given a German accent. Evil Genius: He invents devices for Cy-Kill's schemes. Herr Doktor: He's the Renegades'Mad Scientistand he speaks in a German accent. High-Class Glass: He has a monocle. Names to Run Away from Really Fast: Not his name in the show, but the name his toy was packaged under: Herr Fiend.

characters.name: Bug Bite
characters.description: Adaptational Heroism: He's significantly more noble in theGoBotscomic, even becoming Road Ranger's second in command when he united theGoBotsand chose to free the Earth from Gobotron. Adaptation Dye-Job: His original toy and cartoon version were yellow, but since he transforms into a VW beetle, his appearances in Transformers media have given him a white/silver color scheme to distinguish him from Bumblebee. Ascended Extra: He was just a one shot character in the cartoon, he's become the single most prominentGoBotin Transformers lore. The Dragon: To Gunnyr.

characters.name: Zero
characters.description: The former leader of the Renegades before Cy-Kill seized control. He eventually broke away to form his own third faction. Eviler than Thou: It's hard to say that he's actually more evil than Cy-Kill, since Cyke attempts all manner of mass murder on a regular basis; but personality-wise, where Cy-Kill at least comes across asAffably Evilto his own troops, everything about Zero communicates that he is truly anastypiece of work. No wonder the Renegades were so willing to switch their loyalty to Cy-Kill. The Resenter: He did not like serving under Cy-Kill one bit, and eventually left to form his own army. The Starscream:Played With. While he's the closest thing to this archetype in the series, unlike Starscream, he successfullydoessomething about his situation and leaves to form his own army. What Happened to the Mouse?: Sadly, after the show makes a pretty big deal of Zero forming a "Third Column," it's the last we hear of him in the series.

characters.name: Scorp and Vamp
characters.description: Beware My Stinger Tail: Scorp's alt-mode is a scorpion monster, and his robot mode keeps the stinger tail. Non-Standard Character Design: Designed with a monster-themed motif, rather than transforming into standard vehicles. Really 700 Years Old: While this trope probably doesn't apply to mostGoBots(they regard ancient Egypt as legitimately a very long time ago), these two reallyarethat old, dating back to the first generation of Gobots to be cyborgized. Villainous Friendship: With Pincher, a similarly-designed modern Renegade who ended up being the first outsider to contact them in millenia.

characters.name: Zod
characters.description: The Juggernaut: Zods really areridiculouslytough opponents. Leader-One gets absolutelymauledby one early on in the series. Mecha-Mooks: They're nonsentient machines, so if you actuallycandestroy one, nobody's going to mourn it. A whole armada of them gets blown up in episode 5.

characters.name: Matt Hunter
characters.description: An astronaut who becomes one of the Guardians' first and most prominent human contacts on Earth. Big Brother Mentor: He acts as an older brother figure to Nick and AJ. Porn Stache: Matt sports a big ol' 1980's moustache.

characters.name: Nick Burns
characters.description: Heterosexual Life-Partners: He forms a close friendship with Scooter.

characters.name: Zebediah Braxis
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He tends to get humiliated on a regular basis.He deserves it, too.Sadly, over time,this takes its toll. Expy: Clearly based on Dr. Zachary Smith fromLost in Space,butwithoutDr. Smith'shidden heart of gold. Mad Scientist: He's a demented scientist who uses his genius to aid the Renegades. The Quisling: He is willing to betray humanity by siding with the Renegades. Sanity Slippage: While he was always treasonous, by his last appearance, he's gone stark raving mad.

characters.name: The Insectoids
characters.description: All There in the Manual: Queen Exor's name is only revealed in the episode recap. Ambiguous Robots: They look mechanical but have a clear insect theme. Muddling matters is they originated from a parallel dimension. Gone Horribly Right: Cy-Kill summoned them to Earth for their strength and power, except they proved too powerful to control and almost succeeded in taking out both factions. Hive Caste System: The insectoids first arrive with the queen and her drones but after getting control of the interphaser she summons her soldiers who prove to be a lot tougher. Insect Queen: They are led Queen Exor, whose able to manipulate matter with her mind.

characters.name: Anya Turgonova
characters.description: Ambiguously Brown: Asian features with a European-sounding name, she's presumably of mixed ancestry (which is entirely plausible if she's Russian). Dirty Commies: Averted, which is impressive forThe '80s. Although the political situation makes for friction early on, she's definitely one of the good guys.

characters.name: The Master Renegade
characters.description: Big Eater: Spending ages in suspended animation left him with a big appetite, which the Master Renegades satisfied with the unsuspecting Go-Bots hospitality. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Initially plays along with the Go-Bots assumption he was the Last Engineer, but turns on them the moment the Renegades show up. Eyepatch of Power: Possess a cybernetic eye which can shoot out a laser.

characters.name: Wrecks
characters.description: Heroic Neutral: Rough around the edges, but a good guy. Still, he doesn't give a flip about the fight between the Guardians and the Renegades. He and his people have their own problems. King of the Homeless: A leader among the poor wretches of Old Gobotron. Wrong Side of the Tracks: Old Gobotron is an absolutely miserable slum. It's notable that one of the only true on-screen deaths in the series is an alien who dies in this episode.
